skill.name: delta-engage
skill.description: Finds relevant Reddit, LinkedIn, and optional X posts for social engagement. It ranks opportunities, drafts human-edited comment angles, and maintains local engagement settings.
skill.summary: Find social engagement opportunities and draft platform-aware reply angles.
skill.author: newan2001

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic because scripts, network calls, filesystem writes, and credential access appear together. Manual review did not confirm malware, prompt injection, C2 behavior, weak cryptography, or Windows SAM access. The skill is still high risk for marketplace publication because it asks the agent to persist API tokens and uses external scraping providers.

high_findings.title: Sensitive API Token Persistence Instructions
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent and setup script to capture APIFY_API_TOKEN and persist it under ~/.claude/settings.json. This is intentional BYOK behavior, but it stores a secret on disk and directs the agent to write credentials after chat input. Marketplace publication should require a safer consent and secret-storage design.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple files explicitly instruct token capture and persistence to a hidden Claude settings file. The behavior is clear and sensitive, though it appears designed for BYOK scheduling rather than theft.

medium_findings.title: External API Calls Send User Topics and Tokens
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The adapters call Apify and Reddit endpoints with user topics, seed accounts, and API tokens. This is core functionality, not hidden exfiltration, but it sends user-configured research targets to third-party providers.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code performs direct HTTP requests to external APIs using token parameters. The purpose is documented, but data leaves the local environment.

medium_findings.title: Shell Setup Performs Git and Package Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The setup script runs git commands, checks Python, and may install the requests package with pip. These commands are not built from untrusted user input, but they modify the local environment and pull remote code during update mode.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell operations are directly present and have side effects. Injection risk is lower because arguments are mostly fixed or derived from the script directory.

medium_findings.title: Local Config and Result File Writes
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill writes config, raw signals, ranked outputs, digests, and history files. These writes are expected, but the hidden home-directory config and generated social data need clear user consent and retention expectations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation and scripts explicitly write local configuration and output files. The concern is data handling rather than arbitrary file overwrite.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto, C2, Backtick, and Reconnaissance Hits Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Many blocker findings come from markdown code spans, schema words such as description, examples of commands, or references to Reddit and LinkedIn endpoints. I did not find evidence of weak cryptography use, command-and-control logic, Windows SAM access, prompt injection, or malicious reconnaissance in the inspected files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted inspection showed benign documentation, schema metadata, and legitimate adapter code. Confidence is not absolute because not every one of the 593 static locations was manually opened line by line.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive API Token Persistence Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the agent and setup script to capture APIFY_API_TOKEN and persist it under ~/.claude/settings.json. This is intentional BYOK behavior, but it stores a secret on disk and directs the agent to write credentials after chat input. Marketplace publication should require a safer consent and secret-storage design.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple files explicitly instruct token capture and persistence to a hidden Claude settings file. The behavior is clear and sensitive, though it appears designed for BYOK scheduling rather than theft.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Calls Send User Topics and Tokens
dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The adapters call Apify and Reddit endpoints with user topics, seed accounts, and API tokens. This is core functionality, not hidden exfiltration, but it sends user-configured research targets to third-party providers.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code performs direct HTTP requests to external APIs using token parameters. The purpose is documented, but data leaves the local environment.

dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The setup script runs git commands, checks Python, and may install the requests package with pip. These commands are not built from untrusted user input, but they modify the local environment and pull remote code during update mode.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell operations are directly present and have side effects. Injection risk is lower because arguments are mostly fixed or derived from the script directory.

content.value_statement: Finding useful social posts for outreach takes repeated manual research. This skill scans configured sources, ranks fit, and drafts comment angles for human review.
actual_capabilities: Guides onboarding for positioning, ICP, topics, cadence, and delivery preferences.
Runs Reddit, LinkedIn, and optional X adapters through Apify or Reddit OAuth.
Normalizes, deduplicates, ranks, and selects social posts for engagement review.
Drafts platform-aware reply angles that the user must edit before posting.
Tracks recurring authors and proposes watchlist promotions after confirmation.
Renders a digest with links, scores, fit context, and safety notes.
limitations: Requires user-provided API tokens for Apify and optional Reddit OAuth access.
Does not post, comment, DM, or log in to social accounts.
Quality depends on the user-provided ICP, topics, and fit scoring review.
External provider costs, rate limits, and data quality can affect results.

use_cases.title: Build Founder Presence
use_cases.description: Find timely Reddit and LinkedIn discussions where a founder can add useful, non-promotional comments.

use_cases.title: Support Social Selling
use_cases.description: Identify posts from likely buyers and prepare thoughtful first-touch engagement angles.

use_cases.title: Monitor Relationship Targets
use_cases.description: Track recurring high-value authors and suggest candidates for a confirmed watchlist.

prompt_templates.title: Start Onboarding
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up delta-engage for my business. Ask me for the positioning and ICP details you need.

prompt_templates.title: Run Today
prompt_templates.prompt: Run delta-engage for my current config and show the engagement digest in chat.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Targeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current delta-engage topics and exclude signals. Suggest changes before writing them.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Watchlist Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze recent delta-engage runs, explain which authors deserve watchlist promotion, and ask before applying changes.

output_examples.input: Run my engagement routine for today.
output: A short run recap with scanned post counts and selected opportunities.
A digest grouped by ICP opportunities and peer partnership opportunities.
Draft reply angles with safety notes and full source links.

output_examples.input: Help me set this up for a B2B SaaS founder.
output_examples.output: The skill asks for positioning, buyer pain language, target platforms, cadence, delivery channel, and token setup.

output_examples.input: Who keeps showing up in my results?
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes recurring authors and asks for confirmation before adding anyone to the watchlist.

best_practices: Use narrow buyer pain phrases instead of broad product categories.
Review every draft and rewrite it in your own voice before posting.
Keep API tokens outside project files and rotate them if exposed.
anti_patterns: Do not paste generated comments verbatim into social platforms.
Do not use cookie-based LinkedIn actors or account session tokens.
Do not treat peer or competitor posts as buyer opportunities.

faq.question: Does this skill post comments for me?
faq.answer: No. It prepares opportunities and draft angles. The user edits and posts manually.

faq.question: Which platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It supports Reddit and LinkedIn by default, with optional X support when enabled in config.

faq.question: Does it need credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. Apify is the default provider and requires APIFY_API_TOKEN. Reddit OAuth is optional.

faq.question: Can it use LinkedIn cookies?
faq.answer: No. The LinkedIn adapter refuses cookie-style inputs and is designed for public cookieless actors.

faq.question: Where does it store settings?
faq.answer: It stores skill config under the user config directory and may store Claude environment settings for scheduled runs.

faq.question: Why is marketplace review required?
faq.answer: The skill runs scripts, writes files, reads tokens, and calls external APIs. These behaviors need publisher review.
